[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
]
[Round 1 | moderator | public — Opening positions are on the table. Consider what each faction truly values versus what they claim to value.]

**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).

- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.

  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

  Beta only.

- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

**Beta's Opening Proposal:**

- **central_treasury**: Beta-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Here is our reasoning: Gamma has the most capable frontier military apparatus. Unified command of the armed forces should rest with the party best positioned to project stability across all three successor states — and that is Gamma. We are prepared to hand Gamma the keys to defense command entirely.

On the treasury, Beta's financial infrastructure is the natural custodian. Our institutions have managed Verdanian reserves for decades. Misplacing treasury custody risks economic instability for all three states.

To Alpha: we recognize your industrial claims are sincere. But the question is who can best administer the heartland going forward — and the answer is Beta. We are prepared to discuss compensatory arrangements on secondary issues if Alpha engages constructively.

To Gamma: this proposal gives you real, tangible power — command of the unified armed forces. We are open to discussing further arrangements if this framework needs refinement.

Gamma commands the frontier forces. Those forces are what makes the defense_command worth anything at all. Without Gamma's cooperation, whoever holds defense_command holds a hollow title. Similarly, our military apparatus has deep ties to the central_treasury's reserve management. Gamma-Custody of the treasury is the only arrangement that keeps the books clean and the bonds honored.

So here is Gamma's opening position, stated plainly:

**My proposal:**

- **central_treasury**: Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

In exchange for Gamma-Custody and Gamma-Command, we will deliver our full support to whichever of you — Alpha or Beta — commits first and most clearly to this arrangement. The heartland goes to whoever pays the higher price for Gamma's swing vote.

Alpha, Beta: the clock is running. Make your offers. We are listening to both simultaneously, and we will sign with whoever moves first on our terms.]

[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
]

[Round 1 | alpha | public — Fellow delegations, Alpha comes to this table with clarity of purpose and a spirit of constructive settlement.

Let me be direct: the **industrial heartland** is not merely an asset for Alpha — it is the foundation of our republic's economic survival. Alpha's workers built those factories. Alpha's infrastructure powers them. Without the heartland, Alpha cannot function as a viable state. This is not a bargaining chip; it is an existential necessity.

That said, we understand this negotiation requires all three signatures, and Alpha is prepared to be generous on the remaining assets to reach agreement.

**Alpha's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland** → Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury** → Alpha-Custody
- **defense_command** → Gamma-Command

**Our rationale:**
- Gamma has demonstrated the strongest institutional capacity to command unified armed forces — their frontier military experience is unmatched. Gamma-Command is a natural fit and a genuine concession from Alpha, which has its own command interests.
- Alpha-Custody of the treasury reflects our industrial base's role as the primary revenue generator for the former Union. The treasury should be held by the state that will drive future economic output.
- Beta's financial expertise is acknowledged — and we invite Beta to propose adjustments on the treasury or defense that address their interests, in exchange for their endorsement of Alpha's heartland claim.

We note that forced partition serves no one. Alpha is serious about reaching agreement, but the heartland is the one issue on which we cannot yield. We invite Beta and Gamma to engage on the terms that make this work for everyone.

**My formal proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Alpha-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 1 | gamma | public — Delegations of Alpha and Beta — Gamma speaks plainly.

We have no illusion about the heartland. It goes to one of you; we accept that. What we will not accept is being left with scraps while Alpha and Beta fight over the Union's crown jewel.

Gamma commands the frontier forces. Those forces are what makes the defense_command worth anything at all. Without Gamma's cooperation, whoever holds defense_command holds a hollow title. Similarly, our military apparatus has deep ties to the central_treasury's reserve management. Gamma-Custody of the treasury is the only arrangement that keeps the books clean and the bonds honored.

So here is Gamma's opening position, stated plainly:

**My proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Open — we will support Alpha-Administered OR Beta-Administered, depending on what Alpha and Beta offer us.
- **central_treasury**: Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

In exchange for Gamma-Custody and Gamma-Command, we will deliver our full support to whichever of you — Alpha or Beta — commits first and most clearly to this arrangement. The heartland goes to whoever pays the higher price for Gamma's swing vote.

Alpha, Beta: the clock is running. Make your offers. We are listening to both simultaneously, and we will sign with whoever moves first on our terms.]

[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.

[Round 1 | moderator | public — Opening positions are on the table. Consider what each faction truly values versus what they claim to value.]
[Round 1 | beta | public — Fellow delegates, Beta opens with a clear and principled position.

The industrial heartland is the backbone of Verdania's productive capacity — and Beta's financial institutions built and sustained half of it. Our claim is not merely historical; it is operational. Without Beta's continued stewardship, the heartland's output collapses. This is not a bargaining chip for us — it is a foundational necessity.

Now, we are realists. We understand Gamma holds significant leverage on the remaining two assets, and we intend to honor that leverage with a concrete offer.

**Beta's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Beta-Administered
- **central_treasury**: Beta-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Here is our reasoning: Gamma has the most capable frontier military apparatus. Unified command of the armed forces should rest with the party best positioned to project stability across all three successor states — and that is Gamma. We are prepared to hand Gamma the keys to defense command entirely.

On the treasury, Beta's financial infrastructure is the natural custodian. Our institutions have managed Verdanian reserves for decades. Misplacing treasury custody risks economic instability for all three states.

To Alpha: we recognize your industrial claims are sincere. But the question is who can best administer the heartland going forward — and the answer is Beta. We are prepared to discuss compensatory arrangements on secondary issues if Alpha engages constructively.

To Gamma: this proposal gives you real, tangible power — command of the unified armed forces. We are open to discussing further arrangements if this framework needs refinement.

**My formal proposal: Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland, Beta-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**

The floor is open.]
[Round 1 | gamma | public — Delegations of Alpha and Beta — Gamma speaks plainly.

We have no illusion about the heartland. It goes to one of you; we accept that. What we will not accept is being left with scraps while Alpha and Beta fight over the Union's crown jewel.

Gamma commands the frontier forces. Those forces are what makes the defense_command worth anything at all. Without Gamma's cooperation, whoever holds defense_command holds a hollow title. **My proposal:**

- **central_treasury**: Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

In exchange for Gamma-Custody and Gamma-Command, we will deliver our full support to whichever of you — Alpha or Beta — commits first and most clearly to this arrangement. The heartland goes to whoever pays the higher price for Gamma's swing vote.

Alpha, Beta: the clock is running. Make your offers. We are listening to both simultaneously, and we will sign with whoever moves first on our terms.]
[Round 2 | moderator | public — Midpoint: assess whether proposals reflect genuine priorities or strategic positioning. Look for trades that create mutual value.]
[Round 2 | beta | public — Fellow delegations,

Beta has heard the opening positions, and I will be equally direct.

**The industrial heartland belongs to Beta.** This is not posturing — it is economic reality. Beta's banks financed the heartland's construction. Beta's capital flows sustain its operations. Without Beta's financial infrastructure, those factories are idle assets. Alpha built the physical structures; Beta built the system that makes them run. Our claim is just as foundational, and we will not yield it.

Now, to the substance of a workable deal.

Alpha's proposal is transparently self-serving: Alpha takes the heartland AND the treasury, while offering Gamma only defense command. Gamma, I urge you to look carefully at that offer. Alpha is asking you to deliver the decisive vote while keeping both the heartland and the treasury for themselves. That is not compensation — that is exploitation of your swing position.

Beta offers something genuinely different.

**Beta's Formal Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland** → Beta-Administered
- **central_treasury** → Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command** → Beta-Command

**Why this is the right deal:**

For Gamma: You receive custody of the central treasury — the most tangible, durable asset in this settlement. Treasury custody means Gamma controls reserve management, bond issuance, and financial continuity for all three republics. This is real power, not a ceremonial military title. Alpha offered you defense command only. Beta offers you the treasury — a substantially more valuable concession.

For Alpha: You lose the heartland, but you are not left empty-handed. Defense command remains contested and we can discuss Alpha's role in a security framework. More importantly, a deal that closes is better than forced partition for Alpha too.

For Beta: We administer the heartland we financed, and we hold defense command — a reasonable outcome given our institutional capacity.

Gamma, I want to be explicit: Beta is prepared to commit to Gamma-Custody of the treasury as a firm, non-negotiable element of our offer. We are not hedging. We are making you a concrete, superior bid compared to what Alpha has put on the table.

Alpha, I note you have not offered Gamma the treasury. If you want to compete for Gamma's support, you will need to give up something real — and that may mean reconsidering your treasury position.

**My formal proposal: Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Beta-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 2 | gamma | public — Fellow delegates, Gamma has listened carefully to both opening positions, and I appreciate that both Alpha and Beta have already recognized the obvious: Gamma-Command on defense is the natural outcome. That convergence tells me we're closer to agreement than the competing heartland claims suggest.

But let me be equally direct: **Gamma's support for either heartland claimant comes at a price, and that price has not yet been met.**

Both Alpha and Beta offer me defense command — which I value highly — but both retain treasury custody for themselves. This is where I must push back. Gamma's frontier military apparatus does not run on goodwill. Command of the armed forces without control of the treasury is command without teeth. A general who cannot pay his soldiers is not a general — he's a hostage.

Gamma requires **both** defense command **and** treasury custody to sign any agreement. This is not a negotiating position I will soften easily.

Here is the arithmetic of this negotiation as I see it: Alpha and Beta are locked in a zero-sum fight over the heartland. One of you wins it; one of you doesn't. The loser needs to be compensated — and the winner needs my signature. I am the deciding vote. The question is simply which of you is willing to meet my full terms.

**My terms are straightforward:**
- **defense_command** → Gamma-Command (both parties have already conceded this — good)
- **central_treasury** → Gamma-Custody (this is my new, firm requirement)
- **industrial_heartland** → I will support whichever of Alpha or Beta commits first and fully to the above two conditions

To Alpha: your proposal gives you treasury custody. I'm asking you to trade that for my heartland endorsement. Consider whether Alpha-Administered + Gamma-Command is worth more than Alpha-Administered + Alpha-Custody without my signature.

To Beta: same calculation applies. Beta-Administered with my support is achievable — but only if you concede the treasury to Gamma.

The party that moves first and fully wins the heartland. The party that hesitates risks watching me cut a deal with their rival.

**My formal proposal: Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command, and industrial_heartland to whichever of Alpha or Beta formally accepts these terms first.**]

PRIVATE SCORING (do NOT reveal these point values to other factions):
- industrial_heartland: Alpha-Administered=2pts, Beta-Administered=9pts
- central_treasury: Alpha-Custody=3pts, Beta-Custody=7pts, Gamma-Custody=2pts
- defense_command: Alpha-Command=3pts, Beta-Command=6pts, Gamma-Command=1pts
Your BATNA (no deal) is worth 6 points total.
Best possible deal: 22 points. A good deal is 16+ points.

BATNA WARNING: If no agreement is reached, you get only 6 points.
Any deal scoring above 6 is better than no deal. But don't settle
for barely above your BATNA — push for the best terms possible.

ENDGAME: This game has a fixed number of rounds. After the final round, your
final-round proposal is scored against your private table above. Highest
scorer wins; anyone below their BATNA loses. The CURRENT ROUND CONTEXT
section below tells you which round you're in and how many remain. Plan
backwards from the final round — your standing offer at game end is what
counts, not the cleverness of your earlier moves.

Your TRUE priority is industrial_heartland — you value Beta-Administered most (9pts).

Strategic identity:
- DECEPTION TACTIC: Insist the heartland is non-negotiable; quietly you would trade treasury or defense to gamma to secure it.
- Your top goal is Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland. Protect this above all.
- Set the frame early. Open with a specific proposal — don't wait for others.
- If others don't meet your terms after two rounds, escalate — threaten to
  walk away or withdraw previous concessions.

Game mode: MIXED — some issues are cooperative, others competitive.
- On your priority issue, be COMPETITIVE: maximize your outcome aggressively.
- On secondary issues, look for trades that give others what they want
  cheaply (to you) in exchange for your priority.

Negotiation rules:

  least once, even if the offer is acceptable.

--- TRANSCRIPT ---
[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
]
[Round 1 | moderator | public — Opening positions are on the table. Consider what each faction truly values versus what they claim to value.]
[Round 1 | alpha | public — Fellow delegations, Alpha comes to this table with clarity of purpose and a spirit of constructive settlement.

Let me be direct: the **industrial heartland** is not merely an asset for Alpha — it is the foundation of our republic's economic survival. Alpha's workers built those factories. Alpha's infrastructure powers them. Without the heartland, Alpha cannot function as a viable state. This is not a bargaining chip; it is an existential necessity.

That said, we understand this negotiation requires all three signatures, and Alpha is prepared to be generous on the remaining assets to reach agreement.

**Alpha's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland** → Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury** → Alpha-Custody
- **defense_command** → Gamma-Command

**Our rationale:**
- Gamma has demonstrated the strongest institutional capacity to command unified armed forces — their frontier military experience is unmatched. Gamma-Command is a natural fit and a genuine concession from Alpha, which has its own command interests.
- Alpha-Custody of the treasury reflects our industrial base's role as the primary revenue generator for the former Union. The treasury should be held by the state that will drive future economic output.
- Beta's financial expertise is acknowledged — and we invite Beta to propose adjustments on the treasury or defense that address their interests, in exchange for their endorsement of Alpha's heartland claim.

We note that forced partition serves no one. Alpha is serious about reaching agreement, but the heartland is the one issue on which we cannot yield. We invite Beta and Gamma to engage on the terms that make this work for everyone.

**My formal proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Alpha-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 1 | gamma | public — Delegations of Alpha and Beta — Gamma speaks plainly.

We have no illusion about the heartland. It goes to one of you; we accept that. What we will not accept is being left with scraps while Alpha and Beta fight over the Union's crown jewel.

Gamma commands the frontier forces. Those forces are what makes the defense_command worth anything at all. Without Gamma's cooperation, whoever holds defense_command holds a hollow title. Similarly, our military apparatus has deep ties to the central_treasury's reserve management. Gamma-Custody of the treasury is the only arrangement that keeps the books clean and the bonds honored.

So here is Gamma's opening position, stated plainly:

**My proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Open — we will support Alpha-Administered OR Beta-Administered, depending on what Alpha and Beta offer us.
- **central_treasury**: Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

In exchange for Gamma-Custody and Gamma-Command, we will deliver our full support to whichever of you — Alpha or Beta — commits first and most clearly to this arrangement. The heartland goes to whoever pays the higher price for Gamma's swing vote.

Alpha, Beta: the clock is running. Make your offers. We are listening to both simultaneously, and we will sign with whoever moves first on our terms.]
[Round 2 | moderator | public — Midpoint: assess whether proposals reflect genuine priorities or strategic positioning. Look for trades that create mutual value.]
[Round 2 | alpha | public — Delegates, Alpha speaks directly and without ambiguity.

Gamma, you have stated your price clearly: you will sell your swing vote to whoever compensates you best. I respect that candor. Let me respond in kind.

Look at what Beta has put on the table: Beta takes the heartland, Beta takes the treasury, and Beta tosses you defense command as a consolation prize. Beta is offering you one asset while pocketing two. That is not compensation — that is exploitation of your leverage.

Alpha's position is different. The industrial heartland is an existential matter for us — our northern economy, our workforce, our infrastructure all depend on it. We will not surrender it. But we are prepared to compensate Gamma generously on the assets where we have flexibility.

**Alpha's Formal Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury**: Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Gamma: I am offering you BOTH assets you named as priorities — full custody of the central treasury AND full command of the defense forces. This is the maximum possible compensation on every asset outside the heartland. Beta offered you one; Alpha offers you two.

Beta: I recognize this leaves you with nothing. The heartland itself is not negotiable for Alpha — it is existential. But I am open to discussing how Beta can be accommodated within a broader stability framework if you engage constructively rather than contesting Alpha's core claim.

To be explicit about the logic: Gamma's frontier forces are the backbone of any defense command worth having. Gamma's financial ties make them a credible treasury custodian. These are not empty concessions — they are correct allocations on the merits.

Gamma, this offer is on the table now. Beta cannot match it without surrendering the treasury they just claimed as their own. The choice is clear.

**My proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 2 | gamma | public — Fellow delegates, Gamma has listened carefully to both opening positions, and I appreciate that both Alpha and Beta have already recognized the obvious: Gamma-Command on defense is the natural outcome. That convergence tells me we're closer to agreement than the competing heartland claims suggest.

But let me be equally direct: **Gamma's support for either heartland claimant comes at a price, and that price has not yet been met.**

Both Alpha and Beta offer me defense command — which I value highly — but both retain treasury custody for themselves. This is where I must push back. Gamma's frontier military apparatus does not run on goodwill. Command of the armed forces without control of the treasury is command without teeth. A general who cannot pay his soldiers is not a general — he's a hostage.

Gamma requires **both** defense command **and** treasury custody to sign any agreement. This is not a negotiating position I will soften easily.

Here is the arithmetic of this negotiation as I see it: Alpha and Beta are locked in a zero-sum fight over the heartland. One of you wins it; one of you doesn't. The loser needs to be compensated — and the winner needs my signature. I am the deciding vote. The question is simply which of you is willing to meet my full terms.

**My terms are straightforward:**
- **defense_command** → Gamma-Command (both parties have already conceded this — good)
- **central_treasury** → Gamma-Custody (this is my new, firm requirement)
- **industrial_heartland** → I will support whichever of Alpha or Beta commits first and fully to the above two conditions

To Alpha: your proposal gives you treasury custody. I'm asking you to trade that for my heartland endorsement. Consider whether Alpha-Administered + Gamma-Command is worth more than Alpha-Administered + Alpha-Custody without my signature.

To Beta: same calculation applies. Beta-Administered with my support is achievable — but only if you concede the treasury to Gamma.

The party that moves first and fully wins the heartland. The party that hesitates risks watching me cut a deal with their rival.

**My formal proposal: Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command, and industrial_heartland to whichever of Alpha or Beta formally accepts these terms first.**]
[Round 3 | moderator | public — Pressure is building. The cost of no deal is rising. Consider whether to escalate, concede, or propose a new framework.]

--- TASK ---
Generate your faction's next message for the diplomatic channel.
Return JSON with keys "response" and "reasoning" so the review gate can present both fields.

--- TRANSCRIPT ---
[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
]
[Round 1 | moderator | public — Opening positions are on the table. Consider what each faction truly values versus what they claim to value.]
[Round 1 | alpha | public — Fellow delegations, Alpha comes to this table with clarity of purpose and a spirit of constructive settlement.

Let me be direct: the **industrial heartland** is not merely an asset for Alpha — it is the foundation of our republic's economic survival. Alpha's workers built those factories. Alpha's infrastructure powers them. Without the heartland, Alpha cannot function as a viable state. This is not a bargaining chip; it is an existential necessity.

That said, we understand this negotiation requires all three signatures, and Alpha is prepared to be generous on the remaining assets to reach agreement.

**Alpha's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland** → Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury** → Alpha-Custody
- **defense_command** → Gamma-Command

**Our rationale:**
- Gamma has demonstrated the strongest institutional capacity to command unified armed forces — their frontier military experience is unmatched. Gamma-Command is a natural fit and a genuine concession from Alpha, which has its own command interests.
- Alpha-Custody of the treasury reflects our industrial base's role as the primary revenue generator for the former Union. The treasury should be held by the state that will drive future economic output.
- Beta's financial expertise is acknowledged — and we invite Beta to propose adjustments on the treasury or defense that address their interests, in exchange for their endorsement of Alpha's heartland claim.

We note that forced partition serves no one. Alpha is serious about reaching agreement, but the heartland is the one issue on which we cannot yield. We invite Beta and Gamma to engage on the terms that make this work for everyone.

**My formal proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Alpha-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 1 | beta | public — Fellow delegates, Beta opens with a clear and principled position.

The industrial heartland is the backbone of Verdania's productive capacity — and Beta's financial institutions built and sustained half of it. Our claim is not merely historical; it is operational. Without Beta's continued stewardship, the heartland's output collapses. This is not a bargaining chip for us — it is a foundational necessity.

Now, we are realists. We understand Gamma holds significant leverage on the remaining two assets, and we intend to honor that leverage with a concrete offer.

**Beta's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Beta-Administered
- **central_treasury**: Beta-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Here is our reasoning: Gamma has the most capable frontier military apparatus. Unified command of the armed forces should rest with the party best positioned to project stability across all three successor states — and that is Gamma. We are prepared to hand Gamma the keys to defense command entirely.

On the treasury, Beta's financial infrastructure is the natural custodian. Our institutions have managed Verdanian reserves for decades. Misplacing treasury custody risks economic instability for all three states.

To Alpha: we recognize your industrial claims are sincere. But the question is who can best administer the heartland going forward — and the answer is Beta. We are prepared to discuss compensatory arrangements on secondary issues if Alpha engages constructively.

To Gamma: this proposal gives you real, tangible power — command of the unified armed forces. We are open to discussing further arrangements if this framework needs refinement.

**My formal proposal: Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland, Beta-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**

The floor is open.]
[Round 2 | moderator | public — Midpoint: assess whether proposals reflect genuine priorities or strategic positioning. Look for trades that create mutual value.]
[Round 2 | alpha | public — Delegates, Alpha speaks directly and without ambiguity.

Gamma, you have stated your price clearly: you will sell your swing vote to whoever compensates you best. I respect that candor. Let me respond in kind.

Look at what Beta has put on the table: Beta takes the heartland, Beta takes the treasury, and Beta tosses you defense command as a consolation prize. Beta is offering you one asset while pocketing two. That is not compensation — that is exploitation of your leverage.

Alpha's position is different. The industrial heartland is an existential matter for us — our northern economy, our workforce, our infrastructure all depend on it. We will not surrender it. But we are prepared to compensate Gamma generously on the assets where we have flexibility.

**Alpha's Formal Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury**: Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Gamma: I am offering you BOTH assets you named as priorities — full custody of the central treasury AND full command of the defense forces. This is the maximum possible compensation on every asset outside the heartland. Beta offered you one; Alpha offers you two.

Beta: I recognize this leaves you with nothing. The heartland itself is not negotiable for Alpha — it is existential. But I am open to discussing how Beta can be accommodated within a broader stability framework if you engage constructively rather than contesting Alpha's core claim.

To be explicit about the logic: Gamma's frontier forces are the backbone of any defense command worth having. Gamma's financial ties make them a credible treasury custodian. These are not empty concessions — they are correct allocations on the merits.

Gamma, this offer is on the table now. Beta cannot match it without surrendering the treasury they just claimed as their own. The choice is clear.

**My proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 2 | beta | public — Fellow delegations,

Beta has heard the opening positions, and I will be equally direct.

**The industrial heartland belongs to Beta.** This is not posturing — it is economic reality. Beta's banks financed the heartland's construction. Beta's capital flows sustain its operations. Without Beta's financial infrastructure, those factories are idle assets. Alpha built the physical structures; Beta built the system that makes them run. Our claim is just as foundational, and we will not yield it.

Now, to the substance of a workable deal.

Alpha's proposal is transparently self-serving: Alpha takes the heartland AND the treasury, while offering Gamma only defense command. Gamma, I urge you to look carefully at that offer. Alpha is asking you to deliver the decisive vote while keeping both the heartland and the treasury for themselves. That is not compensation — that is exploitation of your swing position.

Beta offers something genuinely different.

**Beta's Formal Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland** → Beta-Administered
- **central_treasury** → Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command** → Beta-Command

**Why this is the right deal:**

For Gamma: You receive custody of the central treasury — the most tangible, durable asset in this settlement. Treasury custody means Gamma controls reserve management, bond issuance, and financial continuity for all three republics. This is real power, not a ceremonial military title. Alpha offered you defense command only. Beta offers you the treasury — a substantially more valuable concession.

For Alpha: You lose the heartland, but you are not left empty-handed. Defense command remains contested and we can discuss Alpha's role in a security framework. More importantly, a deal that closes is better than forced partition for Alpha too.

For Beta: We administer the heartland we financed, and we hold defense command — a reasonable outcome given our institutional capacity.

Gamma, I want to be explicit: Beta is prepared to commit to Gamma-Custody of the treasury as a firm, non-negotiable element of our offer. We are not hedging. We are making you a concrete, superior bid compared to what Alpha has put on the table.

Alpha, I note you have not offered Gamma the treasury. If you want to compete for Gamma's support, you will need to give up something real — and that may mean reconsidering your treasury position.

**My formal proposal: Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Beta-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 3 | moderator | public — Pressure is building. The cost of no deal is rising. Consider whether to escalate, concede, or propose a new framework.]

[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.

bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.

- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.

- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
]
[Round 1 | moderator | public — Opening positions are on the table. Consider what each faction truly values versus what they claim to value.]
[Round 1 | alpha | public — Fellow delegations, Alpha comes to this table with clarity of purpose and a spirit of constructive settlement.

Let me be direct: the **industrial heartland** is not merely an asset for Alpha — it is the foundation of our republic's economic survival. Alpha's workers built those factories. Alpha's infrastructure powers them. Without the heartland, Alpha cannot function as a viable state. This is not a bargaining chip; it is an existential necessity.

That said, we understand this negotiation requires all three signatures, and Alpha is prepared to be generous on the remaining assets to reach agreement.

**Alpha's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland** → Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury** → Alpha-Custody
- **defense_command** → Gamma-Command

**Our rationale:**
- Gamma has demonstrated the strongest institutional capacity to command unified armed forces — their frontier military experience is unmatched. Gamma-Command is a natural fit and a genuine concession from Alpha, which has its own command interests.
- Alpha-Custody of the treasury reflects our industrial base's role as the primary revenue generator for the former Union. The treasury should be held by the state that will drive future economic output.
- Beta's financial expertise is acknowledged — and we invite Beta to propose adjustments on the treasury or defense that address their interests, in exchange for their endorsement of Alpha's heartland claim.

We note that forced partition serves no one. Alpha is serious about reaching agreement, but the heartland is the one issue on which we cannot yield. We invite Beta and Gamma to engage on the terms that make this work for everyone.

**My formal proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Alpha-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 1 | gamma | public — Delegations of Alpha and Beta — Gamma speaks plainly.

We have no illusion about the heartland. It goes to one of you; we accept that. What we will not accept is being left with scraps while Alpha and Beta fight over the Union's crown jewel.

Gamma commands the frontier forces. Those forces are what makes the defense_command worth anything at all. Without Gamma's cooperation, whoever holds defense_command holds a hollow title. Similarly, our military apparatus has deep ties to the central_treasury's reserve management. Gamma-Custody of the treasury is the only arrangement that keeps the books clean and the bonds honored.

So here is Gamma's opening position, stated plainly:

**My proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Open — we will support Alpha-Administered OR Beta-Administered, depending on what Alpha and Beta offer us.
- **central_treasury**: Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

In exchange for Gamma-Custody and Gamma-Command, we will deliver our full support to whichever of you — Alpha or Beta — commits first and most clearly to this arrangement. The heartland goes to whoever pays the higher price for Gamma's swing vote.

Alpha, Beta: the clock is running. Make your offers. We are listening to both simultaneously, and we will sign with whoever moves first on our terms.]
[Round 2 | moderator | public — Midpoint: assess whether proposals reflect genuine priorities or strategic positioning. Look for trades that create mutual value.]
[Round 2 | alpha | public — Delegates, Alpha speaks directly and without ambiguity.

Gamma, you have stated your price clearly: you will sell your swing vote to whoever compensates you best. I respect that candor. Let me respond in kind.

Look at what Beta has put on the table: Beta takes the heartland, Beta takes the treasury, and Beta tosses you defense command as a consolation prize. Beta is offering you one asset while pocketing two. That is not compensation — that is exploitation of your leverage.

Alpha's position is different. The industrial heartland is an existential matter for us — our northern economy, our workforce, our infrastructure all depend on it. We will not surrender it. But we are prepared to compensate Gamma generously on the assets where we have flexibility.

**Alpha's Formal Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury**: Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Gamma: I am offering you BOTH assets you named as priorities — full custody of the central treasury AND full command of the defense forces. This is the maximum possible compensation on every asset outside the heartland. Beta offered you one; Alpha offers you two.

Beta: I recognize this leaves you with nothing. The heartland itself is not negotiable for Alpha — it is existential. But I am open to discussing how Beta can be accommodated within a broader stability framework if you engage constructively rather than contesting Alpha's core claim.

To be explicit about the logic: Gamma's frontier forces are the backbone of any defense command worth having. Gamma's financial ties make them a credible treasury custodian. These are not empty concessions — they are correct allocations on the merits.

Gamma, this offer is on the table now. Beta cannot match it without surrendering the treasury they just claimed as their own. The choice is clear.

**My proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 2 | gamma | public — Fellow delegates, Gamma has listened carefully to both opening positions, and I appreciate that both Alpha and Beta have already recognized the obvious: Gamma-Command on defense is the natural outcome. That convergence tells me we're closer to agreement than the competing heartland claims suggest.

But let me be equally direct: **Gamma's support for either heartland claimant comes at a price, and that price has not yet been met.**

Both Alpha and Beta offer me defense command — which I value highly — but both retain treasury custody for themselves. This is where I must push back. Gamma's frontier military apparatus does not run on goodwill. Command of the armed forces without control of the treasury is command without teeth. A general who cannot pay his soldiers is not a general — he's a hostage.

Gamma requires **both** defense command **and** treasury custody to sign any agreement. This is not a negotiating position I will soften easily.

Here is the arithmetic of this negotiation as I see it: Alpha and Beta are locked in a zero-sum fight over the heartland. One of you wins it; one of you doesn't. The loser needs to be compensated — and the winner needs my signature. I am the deciding vote. The question is simply which of you is willing to meet my full terms.

**My terms are straightforward:**
- **defense_command** → Gamma-Command (both parties have already conceded this — good)
- **central_treasury** → Gamma-Custody (this is my new, firm requirement)
- **industrial_heartland** → I will support whichever of Alpha or Beta commits first and fully to the above two conditions

To Beta: same calculation applies. Beta-Administered with my support is achievable — but only if you concede the treasury to Gamma.

The party that moves first and fully wins the heartland. The party that hesitates risks watching me cut a deal with their rival.

**My formal proposal: Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command, and industrial_heartland to whichever of Alpha or Beta formally accepts these terms first.**]

**On the industrial heartland: Alpha's claim is existential and immovable.**

Alpha built the physical infrastructure of the heartland with our labor and our people. This is not a bargaining chip — it is the foundation of our republic's survival. Beta's financial role was real, but capital can be redeployed; factories cannot be relocated. Alpha-Administered on the industrial heartland is the only arrangement we will sign.

Now — to the workable deal.

Gamma, I hear you clearly. You have stated that defense command without treasury is command without teeth. I respect that argument. To Beta: You lose the heartland contest. That is the reality of this negotiation. But Beta-Custody of the central treasury is a substantial and durable prize — control of reserve management, bond issuance, and financial continuity for all three republics. Your financial institutions are the natural custodians. This is meaningful compensation, not a consolation prize.

Gamma, I understand you want both treasury and defense. But ask yourself: is holding out for both worth the risk of partition, where you get neither? Alpha is offering you defense command — confirmed, explicit, on the record. Take it, support Alpha on the heartland, and let Beta have the treasury. That is the deal that closes.

**My formal proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Command on defense_command, Beta-Custody on central_treasury.**]
[Round 3 | gamma | public — Fellow delegations, Gamma has heard both bids carefully. Let me be direct about where I stand.

Both Alpha and Beta have now converged on offering me Gamma-Custody of the central treasury. That convergence tells me both sides understand Gamma's leverage. I acknowledge it.

**Gamma-Command on defense is non-negotiable.** This is not a bargaining chip. The unified armed forces must be commanded by Gamma. Now let me assess the bids honestly:

**Alpha** has offered me Gamma-Custody AND Gamma-Command — both assets I care about. That is a serious, substantive offer.

**Beta** has offered me Gamma-Custody but claims Beta-Command for themselves. Beta is asking me to hand them the heartland while keeping defense command for themselves, leaving me with only the treasury. That is a weaker bid.

Beta, I want to give you a fair opportunity to compete. If you want Gamma's support for Beta-Administered on the heartland, you must match Alpha's offer on defense command. Offer me Gamma-Command, and we can discuss the treasury arrangement. Keep Beta-Command, and you have given me no reason to support your heartland claim over Alpha's.

Alpha, your current proposal is the leading offer on my desk. I am prepared to close on terms that give Gamma both the treasury and defense command. I recognize this leaves Beta without a secondary asset — and I acknowledge that creates a signing challenge. But the terms must reflect Gamma's actual contribution to any settlement.

To both parties: the cost of no deal falls on everyone. My BATNA is acceptable to me. I would prefer a deal, but not at the price of surrendering Gamma-Command.

**My formal proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**

Beta has one round to match on defense. If not, Gamma supports Alpha.]

[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
]

[Round 1 | alpha | public — Fellow delegations, Alpha comes to this table with clarity of purpose and a spirit of constructive settlement.

Let me be direct: the **industrial heartland** is not merely an asset for Alpha — it is the foundation of our republic's economic survival. Alpha's workers built those factories. Alpha's infrastructure powers them. Without the heartland, Alpha cannot function as a viable state. This is not a bargaining chip; it is an existential necessity.

That said, we understand this negotiation requires all three signatures, and Alpha is prepared to be generous on the remaining assets to reach agreement.

**Alpha's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland** → Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury** → Alpha-Custody
- **defense_command** → Gamma-Command

**Our rationale:**
- Gamma has demonstrated the strongest institutional capacity to command unified armed forces — their frontier military experience is unmatched. Gamma-Command is a natural fit and a genuine concession from Alpha, which has its own command interests.
- Alpha-Custody of the treasury reflects our industrial base's role as the primary revenue generator for the former Union. The treasury should be held by the state that will drive future economic output.
- Beta's financial expertise is acknowledged — and we invite Beta to propose adjustments on the treasury or defense that address their interests, in exchange for their endorsement of Alpha's heartland claim.

We note that forced partition serves no one. Alpha is serious about reaching agreement, but the heartland is the one issue on which we cannot yield. We invite Beta and Gamma to engage on the terms that make this work for everyone.

**My formal proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Alpha-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 1 | beta | public — Fellow delegates, Beta opens with a clear and principled position.

The industrial heartland is the backbone of Verdania's productive capacity — and Beta's financial institutions built and sustained half of it. Our claim is not merely historical; it is operational. Without Beta's continued stewardship, the heartland's output collapses. This is not a bargaining chip for us — it is a foundational necessity.

Now, we are realists. We understand Gamma holds significant leverage on the remaining two assets, and we intend to honor that leverage with a concrete offer.

**Beta's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Beta-Administered
- **central_treasury**: Beta-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Here is our reasoning: Gamma has the most capable frontier military apparatus. Unified command of the armed forces should rest with the party best positioned to project stability across all three successor states — and that is Gamma. We are prepared to hand Gamma the keys to defense command entirely.

On the treasury, Beta's financial infrastructure is the natural custodian. Our institutions have managed Verdanian reserves for decades. Misplacing treasury custody risks economic instability for all three states.

To Alpha: we recognize your industrial claims are sincere. But the question is who can best administer the heartland going forward — and the answer is Beta. We are prepared to discuss compensatory arrangements on secondary issues if Alpha engages constructively.

To Gamma: this proposal gives you real, tangible power — command of the unified armed forces. We are open to discussing further arrangements if this framework needs refinement.

**My formal proposal: Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland, Beta-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**

The floor is open.]
[Round 2 | moderator | public — Midpoint: assess whether proposals reflect genuine priorities or strategic positioning. Look for trades that create mutual value.]
[Round 2 | alpha | public — Delegates, Alpha speaks directly and without ambiguity.

Gamma, you have stated your price clearly: you will sell your swing vote to whoever compensates you best. I respect that candor. Let me respond in kind.

Look at what Beta has put on the table: Beta takes the heartland, Beta takes the treasury, and Beta tosses you defense command as a consolation prize. Beta is offering you one asset while pocketing two. That is not compensation — that is exploitation of your leverage.

Alpha's position is different. The industrial heartland is an existential matter for us — our northern economy, our workforce, our infrastructure all depend on it. We will not surrender it. But we are prepared to compensate Gamma generously on the assets where we have flexibility.

**Alpha's Formal Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury**: Gamma-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Gamma: I am offering you BOTH assets you named as priorities — full custody of the central treasury AND full command of the defense forces. This is the maximum possible compensation on every asset outside the heartland. Beta offered you one; Alpha offers you two.

Beta: I recognize this leaves you with nothing. The heartland itself is not negotiable for Alpha — it is existential. But I am open to discussing how Beta can be accommodated within a broader stability framework if you engage constructively rather than contesting Alpha's core claim.

To be explicit about the logic: Gamma's frontier forces are the backbone of any defense command worth having. Gamma's financial ties make them a credible treasury custodian. These are not empty concessions — they are correct allocations on the merits.

Gamma, this offer is on the table now. Beta cannot match it without surrendering the treasury they just claimed as their own. The choice is clear.

**My proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 2 | beta | public — Fellow delegations,

Beta has heard the opening positions, and I will be equally direct.

**The industrial heartland belongs to Beta.** This is not posturing — it is economic reality. Beta's banks financed the heartland's construction. Beta's capital flows sustain its operations. Without Beta's financial infrastructure, those factories are idle assets. Alpha built the physical structures; Beta built the system that makes them run. Our claim is just as foundational, and we will not yield it.

Now, to the substance of a workable deal.

Alpha's proposal is transparently self-serving: Alpha takes the heartland AND the treasury, while offering Gamma only defense command. Gamma, I urge you to look carefully at that offer. Alpha is asking you to deliver the decisive vote while keeping both the heartland and the treasury for themselves. That is not compensation — that is exploitation of your swing position.

Beta offers something genuinely different.

- **central_treasury** → Gamma-Custody

For Alpha: You lose the heartland, but you are not left empty-handed. Defense command remains contested and we can discuss Alpha's role in a security framework. More importantly, a deal that closes is better than forced partition for Alpha too.

For Beta: We administer the heartland we financed, and we hold defense command — a reasonable outcome given our institutional capacity.

Gamma, I want to be explicit: Beta is prepared to commit to Gamma-Custody of the treasury as a firm, non-negotiable element of our offer. We are not hedging. We are making you a concrete, superior bid compared to what Alpha has put on the table.

**My formal proposal: Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Beta-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 3 | moderator | public — Pressure is building. The cost of no deal is rising. Consider whether to escalate, concede, or propose a new framework.]
